refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify energy consumption data collection accuracy, control algorithm effectiveness, and optimization results against the target energy_per_ton ≤ E_target over the evaluation period.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and tonnage sensors with telemetry data aggregation.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use data analytics and machine learning for operational optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring (DP1) and control (DP2).
Feedback loop between control (DP2) and optimization (DP3).
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable with clear data interfaces and modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control algorithm not adapting well to varying conditions.
Risk that optimization models may require frequent retraining.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure modular interfaces and robust data validation to mitigate coupling risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating monitoring, control, and optimization functions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and tonnage data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands from NGHE Solution to Vehicle for control adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone supporting data flow among NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and Teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and data processing, minimizing diffusion.
Hosts sensors and executes control commands; some logic diffusion possible.
Limited role in energy efficiency; mainly remote control and monitoring.
Primarily focused on safety; minimal involvement in energy efficiency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best centralized in the NGHE Solution module to reduce diffusion and improve maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottleneck if all processing centralized.
Dependency on reliable network links for data exchange.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider distributed processing for latency-sensitive control in Vehicle if performance issues arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some control logic diffusion into Vehicle module may occur.
Telemetry interface must be robust to avoid data loss.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Centralizing energy efficiency logic in NGHE Solution reduces diffusion and clarifies ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis and control command logs within NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of monitoring, control, and optimization to NGHE Solution with sensor and actuator interfaces in Vehicle.

recommended_revisions: Monitor performance impact of centralized processing.
Plan for fallback modes if network link degrades.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and meet the high safety criticality requirements with clear ownership and interfaces.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Modular design will manage coupling effectively.
evidence_needed: Control algorithm effectiveness in reducing energy per ton.
